title: Bound every network call with explicit timeouts
description: Set connect, read/write, and overall deadlines so a stalled peer cannot hang the caller indefinitely.
body: Use when making any outbound network call. Do not rely on the library default (often infinite) or set only a connect timeout while leaving the read unbounded, because a peer that accepts then stalls will hang the caller forever and tie up a connection. Actions: set a connect timeout, a read/write timeout, and an overall request deadline (or a context/cancellation with a deadline that covers retries); choose values from the operation's SLO. Validation signals include a test where a slow/black-hole server causes the call to fail by the deadline rather than hang, and no thread parked indefinitely on a socket read. Risk boundary: removing or unbounding a timeout on a live client path is an availability regression requiring review.
